source.threadTitle: What do you wish you had known when you replaced your roof?

CASE-003.summary: Homeowners often regret not validating ventilation, decking, bathroom venting, permits, insurance, documentation, materials, cleanup, solar readiness, and project scope before roof replacement.

categories.name: Ventilation
categories.userPainPoint: A new roof may not solve attic heat, moisture, or airflow issues.
categories.rootCause: Ventilation details are often underspecified in roof quotes.
categories.recommendedAction: Review ridge vents, soffit intake, baffles, attic air sealing, and climate needs before signing.

categories.name: Decking / OSB
categories.userPainPoint: Hidden decking replacement can create unexpected cost increases.
categories.rootCause: Decking condition is not fully visible until tear-off.
categories.recommendedAction: Ask for per-sheet pricing, photo proof, and approval process.
categories.roofAiOpportunity: Decking Risk Estimate and Quote Clause Checker

categories.name: Bathroom Venting
categories.userPainPoint: Bathroom fans may vent into the attic or need correct roof routing.
categories.rootCause: Exhaust routing can be outside the roofing scope unless explicitly checked.
categories.recommendedAction: Verify fans vent outdoors and ask whether new roof penetrations or flashing are needed.

categories.name: Permit
categories.userPainPoint: Homeowners may not know whether permits are required or handled.
categories.rootCause: Permit rules vary locally and contractor responsibility may be unclear.
categories.recommendedAction: Confirm permit requirements, who pulls the permit, and inspection sign-off.

categories.name: Insurance
categories.userPainPoint: Insurance claims can create uncertainty around deductibles, premiums, and documentation.
categories.rootCause: Insurance decisions mix contractor incentives, policy risk, and storm damage evidence.
categories.recommendedAction: Document damage, understand claim risk, and consider independent inspection.

categories.name: Documentation
categories.userPainPoint: Homeowners may lack photos, warranties, permits, invoices, and product records after work.
categories.rootCause: Documentation is not always requested before project closeout.
categories.recommendedAction: Request before/during/after photos, warranty docs, permit records, specs, and final invoice.

categories.name: Material Selection
categories.userPainPoint: Homeowners may not understand differences in shingles, underlayment, flashing, and warranties.
categories.rootCause: Quotes often emphasize price instead of system components.
categories.recommendedAction: Compare material specs line by line.

categories.name: Shingle Color
categories.userPainPoint: Color can look different on a full roof than on a small sample.
categories.rootCause: Small samples and online photos do not reflect roof-scale appearance or heat impact.
categories.recommendedAction: View installed examples, check HOA rules, and consider climate effects.

categories.name: Metal Roof
categories.userPainPoint: Metal roof decisions introduce cost, noise, weight, expansion, and installer-skill concerns.
categories.rootCause: Metal roofing has different installation requirements than asphalt.
categories.recommendedAction: Verify installer experience, panel type, underlayment, fastening, warranty, and structure fit.

categories.name: Cleanup
categories.userPainPoint: Homeowners may be left with nails, debris, or property damage.
categories.rootCause: Cleanup expectations may not be written into the contract.
categories.recommendedAction: Require magnetic nail sweep, property protection, debris removal, and final walkthrough.

categories.name: Solar Readiness
categories.userPainPoint: Roof replacement may need to be coordinated with future solar plans.
categories.rootCause: Solar and roofing decisions are often planned separately.
categories.recommendedAction: Decide solar timing before roof work and ask about mounting, conduit, warranty, and roof life.

categories.name: Scope Validation
categories.userPainPoint: Homeowners may not know exactly what is included or excluded in the roof quote.
categories.rootCause: Quotes can hide major differences behind similar total prices.
categories.recommendedAction: Get written scope and compare line items before deposit.

top25HomeownerRegrets: Not checking attic ventilation before replacing shingles.
Not asking whether soffit intake and ridge exhaust are balanced.
Not confirming whether baffles are installed or blocked.
Not asking for per-sheet decking or OSB replacement pricing.
Not getting photo proof before approving decking add-ons.
Not verifying bathroom fans vent outdoors instead of into the attic.
Not clarifying who handles permits and inspections.
Not understanding insurance claim consequences before filing.
Not documenting roof condition before the contractor starts.
Not saving warranty, permit, invoice, and product records.
Not comparing underlayment, flashing, drip edge, and ventilation components.
Not comparing quotes line by line beyond the total price.
Not seeing shingle colors on real homes before choosing.
Not considering heat and climate impact of darker shingles.
Not validating whether metal roofing is appropriate for the home.
Not checking whether the contractor has real experience with the selected material.
Not requiring written cleanup expectations.
Not protecting landscaping, gutters, decks, and driveways during work.
Not requiring magnetic nail cleanup and a final walkthrough.
Not planning roof replacement around future solar installation.
Not asking whether new roof work could affect solar mounting or warranty.
Not clarifying what happens if hidden damage is found.
Not confirming whether flashing, pipe boots, vents, and valleys are replaced or reused.
Not understanding labor, material, workmanship, and manufacturer warranty differences.
Not getting the complete project scope in writing before deposit.
